title: Incident: write a blameless postmortem from notes

baseline.output:
## Summary

**Sev2 incident — refund worker (2026-04-18, 38 min)**
- A hotfix was deployed without running migrations, which broke the refund queue.
- Detection lagged: on-call was paged ~12 minutes after customers reported the issue in Slack.
- Mitigation: worker restarted, queue drained cleanly, no data loss.

## Suggested follow-up work

1. **Prevent deploys without migrations** — add a CI/deploy gate that blocks releases when pending migrations aren't applied, so this can't depend on individual diligence.
2. **Shorten detection time** — add queue-depth / worker-stall alerting so on-call is paged from monitoring, not from customer Slack messages. Target detection well under 12 minutes.
3. **Hotfix process review** — document and enforce the hotfix checklist (migrations, review sign-off, rollback plan).
4. **Blameless postmortem** — run the writeup focused on the system gaps (missing CI check, missing alert, review process) rather than on Carol or Alice individually. Repeated individual error usually points to a missing guardrail.
5. **Review process tune-up** — discuss with the team what would make migration-related risks easier to spot in review (e.g., PR template checkbox, automated label when migrations are touched).

A note on the last bullet in your notes: framing it as "make sure Carol doesn't do this again" is worth reconsidering — the durable fix is the CI gate and alerting, not personal accountability. Happy to help draft the postmortem doc or the CI check if useful.

polymath.output:
# Incident Postmortem — Refund Worker Stall (Sev2)

**Date:** 2026-04-18
**Severity:** Sev2
**Duration:** 38 minutes
**Data loss:** None

## Summary

The refund worker stalled for 38 minutes after a hotfix was deployed without the accompanying database migrations, leaving the queue unable to process jobs. Service was restored by restarting the worker, after which the queue drained normally.

## Timeline

| Time (relative) | Event |
|---|---|
| T+0 | Hotfix deployed to production; deployment process did not run the required migrations, leaving the queue in an inconsistent schema state. |
| T+0 → T+~? | Refund worker begins failing to process jobs; queue depth grows. |
| T+? | Customers report issues in Slack. |
| +12 min after customer reports | On-call paged by monitoring. |
| T+38 min | Worker restarted; queue drains; service restored. |

## Detection Gap

There was a **12-minute gap** between customers reporting the issue in Slack and on-call being paged. Customer-visible symptoms preceded our internal alerting, indicating that monitoring did not detect the stalled worker or growing queue depth as a leading indicator.

## Contributing Factors (system-shaped)

- **Deployment tooling did not enforce migration execution.** The hotfix path allowed code to ship without the schema changes it depended on. The system permitted a deploy that the system itself could not safely run.
- **Code review process lacked a migration-coupling check.** Reviewers had no automated or checklist-based signal that the change required migrations, so the dependency was easy to miss for any reviewer.
- **Alerting lagged customer reports by 12 minutes.** Queue-depth and worker-health signals were not wired to page on-call ahead of user-visible impact.
- **No pre-flight verification between deploy and queue health.** Nothing automatically validated that workers were healthy post-deploy.

## Action Categories

### Prevent
- Block deploys when pending migrations exist for the deploying revision (CI/CD gate).
- Add a "requires migration" label/check that fails review automatically when a PR touches models without a paired migration.
- Require hotfixes to go through the same migration-aware deploy path as standard releases.

### Detect
- Add paging alerts on refund-worker queue depth and job-processing latency, tuned to fire before customer-visible impact.
- Add a worker liveness/heartbeat alert that pages within 2 minutes of stall.
- Create a Slack-to-incident bridge so customer-impact mentions in support channels trigger an on-call signal.

### Mitigate
- Add an automated post-deploy health check that rolls back or pages if workers are unhealthy within N minutes of a deploy.
- Document and script the "restart worker + drain queue" recovery so any on-call can execute it without tribal knowledge.

### Process
- Adopt blameless postmortems as the default; review language in templates to focus on system gaps rather than individuals.
- Add a migration checklist item to the PR template and the deploy runbook.
- Schedule a follow-up review of alerting coverage for all background workers, not just refunds.

## Notes on Framing

This postmortem is blameless: individuals acted in good faith within a system that allowed a code-only hotfix to ship without migrations and did not alert on the resulting queue stall ahead of customers. Remediation targets the deploy pipeline, review tooling, and alerting — not individual behavior.
